What open-source software licenses would be most appropriate for this application.  I want to allow individuals/organizations to freely use and share it, but prevent monitizing a behind a paywall without disclosing source.  Does the latest GPL handlle this?

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-21 15:01:51Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking about open-source software licenses for their cv-builder application. They want:
1. Allow individuals/organizations to freely use and share it
2. Prevent monetizing behind a paywall without disclosing source

They're asking specifically about GPL. Let me think through the relevant licenses.

The key concern is the "network use" loophole in GPL v2/v3 - if someone runs GPLv3 software as a service (SaaS), they don't have to release their modifications because they're not "distributing" the software. This is the "ASP loophole" or "SaaS loophole."

The licenses that address this:


AGPL v3 closes this gap by requiring source disclosure for network-based use, which is exactly what they need. GPL v3 alone won't prevent someone from running it as a service without sharing modifications. SSPL goes even further by requiring all infrastructure and management software to be open-sourced if offered as a service, though it's more controversial and not widely accepted as truly open-source.

Other options like Business Source License and Commons Clause exist but either aren't true open-source licenses or have their own limitations—AGPL v3 is the right choice here since it's OSI-approved and directly addresses the SaaS loophole concern.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-21 15:02:01Z)**_

## License Recommendation: AGPL v3

**GPL v3 does NOT cover your use case.** It has the "SaaS loophole" — if someone runs GPL software as a network service (paywall/SaaS), they never "distribute" it, so they have no obligation to release source code.

### The Right Choice: AGPL v3 (GNU Affero GPL v3)

AGPL v3 closes that loophole with **Section 13**: if someone runs the software over a network and users interact with it, they must make the source available to those users.

| Requirement | GPL v3 | **AGPL v3** |
|---|---|---|
| Share source when distributing | ✅ | ✅ |
| Share source when running as SaaS/paywall | ❌ | ✅ |
| Copyleft (modifications must stay open) | ✅ | ✅ |
| OSI-approved open source | ✅ | ✅ |

### Other Options Worth Knowing

- **SSPL** (MongoDB's license) — even stricter than AGPL: operators must open-source their *entire* service stack. OSI does **not** consider it true open source. Probably overkill here.
- **Commons Clause** — an addendum you bolt onto MIT/Apache that bars selling. Simple but not OSI-approved and legally untested.
- **BSL/BUSL** (HashiCorp style) — converts to open source after N years, allows commercial restrictions initially. Not truly open source.

### Recommendation

**Use AGPL v3.** It is:
- OSI-approved and widely understood
- Directly targets your concern (source disclosure required even for hosted/paywall use)
- Compatible with the rest of the open-source ecosystem

Add this to your `LICENSE` file (standard AGPL v3 text) and a header like:

```
SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later
```

One practical note: if you ever want to sell a commercial license to organizations who don't want AGPL obligations (e.g., embedded use), you can do **dual licensing** — AGPL for open use + a paid commercial license. This is the standard model for projects like Qt, MongoDB (pre-SSPL), etc.

```bash
CV-Builder
Copyright (C) 2026 Gregory R. Warnes

SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify
it under the terms of the GNU Affero General Public License as published
by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or
(at your option) any later version.

This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
GNU Affero General Public License for more details.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DUAL LICENSING
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This software is available under a dual-license model:

  1. OPEN SOURCE (AGPL v3):  Free for individuals, non-profit organizations,
     open-source projects, and any use that complies with the terms of the
     GNU Affero General Public License v3 (see full text below).

     Key implication: if you deploy this software as a network service (e.g.,
     a SaaS product or paywall), you must make the complete corresponding
     source code available to your users under AGPL v3.

  2. COMMERCIAL LICENSE:  Organizations that wish to use, modify, or deploy
     this software in a proprietary or commercial product — without the
     source-disclosure obligations of AGPL v3 — may obtain a separate
     commercial license.

     Contact: greg@warnes.net

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GNU AFFERO GENERAL PUBLIC LICENSE (full text follows)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

HEADER
cat LICENSE.agpl3
} > LICENSE && rm LICENSE.agpl3
```

```html
-->
```
